title: Peppermint foot balm
subtitle: A firm cooling balm for dry heels with peppermint essential oil
excerpt: A firm beeswax balm with coconut oil and shea butter, scented with peppermint essential oil. The high beeswax proportion gives the balm a solid texture suited to rubbing into rough heel skin. Peppermint creates a temporary cooling sensation from the menthol content. Fills one 100 ml tin.
sourceNotes: Tisserand and Young, Essential Oil Safety (2nd ed., 2014): peppermint maximum use rate for body products. Formulators Kitchen foot balm ratios (formulatorskitchen.com, accessed May 2026).

recipe.batchNotes: Double for two tins from one batch. A 100 ml tin takes one batch exactly.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: The balm is ready to use once set. Apply after a bath when skin is soft.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 12 months from making. Peppermint EO fades after 12 to 18 months. Discard if the balm develops a rancid coconut or waxy smell.

glossaryTerms.definition: Peppermint essential oil is high in menthol, which activates cold-sensitive receptors in the skin without actually lowering skin temperature. The cooling sensation is temporary and is stronger at higher dilutions. At 4% of the total base weight it is effective on foot skin without being uncomfortable.

The firmness of a finished balm is controlled by the proportion of beeswax. At 20% beeswax (by weight of the total base) the balm is firm enough to pick up on a fingertip and rub into heel skin without melting from hand heat alone.

content.text: A foot balm needs more structure than a face balm: heel skin is thick and dry, and a soft balm will simply spread over the surface rather than penetrate. The higher beeswax proportion gives
that allows the balm to be pressed into cracked skin with a firm stroke. Coconut oil provides an antimicrobial base that suits feet. Shea butter adds emollient slip.

from the peppermint essential oil is the reason foot balms are pleasant to apply after a long day. Do not use on children under two years of age; peppermint essential oil is not recommended for very young children. Do not apply peppermint balm to the face or near the eyes.

items.prepNote: Cosmetic-grade beeswax pellets. 20% of base weight gives a firm, heel-suitable balm.
Refined coconut oil. Refined has a neutral scent that does not compete with the peppermint.
Unrefined or refined. Adds slip and emollient quality.
4% of base weight (90 g). Add when the blend cools to below 45 degrees Celsius. Do not use undiluted.

content.text: Weigh the beeswax pellets, coconut oil, and shea butter into the heatproof metal bowl. Set over a pan of simmering water and stir occasionally until fully melted. Beeswax melts last; stir until no pellets remain. Remove from the heat.

Allow the blend to cool to below 45 degrees Celsius, then stir in the peppermint essential oil. Pour into the tin immediately; peppermint EO contains volatile top notes that will evaporate quickly in a warm room. Fill to just below the tin rim.

Leave undisturbed at room temperature for 1 hour until fully set. The surface may develop a slight white bloom as it cools; this is normal beeswax behaviour and does not indicate a problem. Label with the making date.

Store in a cool place. The balm keeps for 12 months from the making date. The peppermint scent is the first to fade; the balm is still effective as a plain moisturiser after the scent goes. Discard if the balm smells rancid.
